title: Read Parking Signs
summary: Understand street parking signs to know when and where parking is allowed.
learnerPromise: You will be able to read street parking signs and determine whether you can park in a spot legally and for how long.
whyItMatters: Understanding parking signs helps you avoid tickets, towing, and fines. It also makes you more confident when driving or riding with someone who needs to park.
realLifeUses: Figuring out if you can park on a street while visiting a friend
Checking time limits before leaving your car to run errands
Understanding street cleaning or snow emergency restrictions
Helping a driver read signs when you are a passenger
result: You can explain what a parking sign means and whether parking is allowed at a given time.
youWillLearn: How to identify time limits and restricted hours on parking signs
How to recognize permit-only or paid parking zones
How to spot temporary restrictions like street cleaning or loading zones
How to check multiple signs on the same block for conflicting rules
miniChallenge: Walk or drive down a street with parking signs and read at least two of them. For each sign, state whether parking is allowed right now and what restrictions apply.
steps: Locate the parking sign nearest to the spot you are considering.
Read the hours and days shown on the sign carefully.
Check for any additional signs nearby that add or change the rules.
Match the current day and time to the restrictions on the sign.
Decide if parking is allowed and for how long, or move to a different spot.
completionCriteria: You correctly interpreted at least two parking signs
You stated whether parking was allowed at the current time and explained why
commonProblems: Multiple signs on one block can seem to conflict or be confusing
Arrow directions on signs may not be clear about which spots they apply to
Temporary paper signs may add rules not shown on the main sign
tips: Take a photo of the sign in case you need to double-check the rules later
Look up and down the block for additional signs that might affect your spot
When in doubt, choose a different spot with clearer or more relaxed rules
